dataset.description: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.

columns.description: Unique identifier for a customer in the telco service.
columns.name: customer_id

columns.description: Satisfaction score given by the customer, ranging from 1 (low) to 5 (high).
columns.name: satisfaction_score

columns.description: Customer Lifetime Value index, projecting the revenue generated by the customer over the long term.

desc: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.
goal: Use customer demographics, satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV to predict which customers are likely to churn in the coming months, helping the company implement retention strategies.
name: Visual Analytics System telco_goal2_1742639914

plan.path: The user browses View 1 at the [Overview] level and learns about the overall distribution of customers grouped by age and satisfaction categories. Next, the user selects an age category and satisfaction group of interest in View 1, affecting View 2. The user observes the churn score distribution in View 2 and explores specific churn score bins, affecting View 3. Then, in View 3 at the [Detail] level, the user identifies clusters of customers or anomalies. Finally, the user gains actionable insights about high-risk age groupings and churn scores in View 4 at the [Insight] level.

effect.description: Filters data for the selected age category and satisfaction group, narrowing views in subsequent exploration.

visualization.description: A grouped bar chart to visualize the distribution of age categories and their satisfaction levels, allowing observation of demographic factors and satisfaction metrics.

effect.description: Highlight the detailed distribution of customer statuses for specific churn score bins.

visualization.description: A bar chart to observe the distribution of churn scores segmented by customer statuses (Stayed, Churned, Joined).

effect.description: Displays precise data values for satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV tier of the hovered customer.

visualization.description: A scatter plot showing the relationship between satisfaction score and churn score, with points colored by CLTV tiers.
encodings: color: Status_Analysis.cltv (binned into low, medium, high)

visualization.description: A stacked bar chart to show counts of customers grouped by age categories, with bars segmented into churn score bins.

data.reason: Understanding how customer demographics align with satisfaction, churn scores, and CLTV can identify patterns and predict churn risk.
data.requirement: Combine data from the `Customer_Info` and `Status_Analysis` tables to provide demographic details (age, senior citizen status, partner status, dependents) along with churn score, satisfaction score, and CLTV for each customer.

data.reason: Provides an overview of churn behavior across the customer base and highlights high-risk areas for retention strategies.
data.requirement: Aggregate churn-related fields (`churn_score`, `churn_value`, `customer_status`) to produce macro-level metrics such as churn rate and distribution of churn score.

data.reason: Binning these variables improves readability in visualizations and allows clustering of customers with similar attributes for predictive insights.
data.requirement: Characterize distribution patterns by grouping customers based on satisfaction score, CLTV, and churn risk into ranges or bins.

data.reason: Exploring how churn risk correlates with satisfaction and revenue across demographic groups provides actionable insights into targeted interventions.
data.requirement: Perform correlation analysis between `satisfaction_score`, `churn_score`, and `CLTV` across distinct demographic groups (e.g., age groups, partner status).

interaction.reason: Filtering helps users focus on specific subsets of customers, refining insights for actionable strategy development.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to filter visualizations (e.g., heat map, scatter plot) based on demographic variables like age group and partner status.

interaction.reason: Drill-down functionality progresses analysis from macro perspectives to micro-level insights, establishing causality or finer patterns.
interaction.requirement: Enable drill-down interactions from high-level views such as pie charts or bar charts to detailed visualizations like scatter plots or box plots.

interaction.reason: Hover interactions make it easier for users to retrieve detailed information for individual points without over-complicating the plot.
interaction.requirement: Provide interactive highlighting in the scatter plot to display additional context (e.g., exact churn score, satisfaction score, and CLTV) on hover.

interaction.reason: Dynamic binning accommodates granular adjustments and invites exploration of different groupings, yielding greater flexibility in analysis.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to dynamically bin numerical variables like `satisfaction_score`, `churn_score`, and `CLTV` to modify ranges and observe distribution changes.

interaction.reason: Sorting organizes information for better comparison, helping identify which demographic categories align with higher churn.
interaction.requirement: Enable sorting and comparisons in bar charts based on churn scores or demographic categories.

visualization.reason: A heat map clearly represents relationships among these numerical variables in a condensed, easily interpretable format.
visualization.requirement: Use a heat map to visualize the correlation between satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV.

visualization.reason: Bar charts make it easier to compare category results and identify demographic groups with higher churn risks.
visualization.requirement: Develop a bar chart to show churn score distributions grouped by demographic categories like `age`, `senior_citizen`, and `partner`.

visualization.reason: A scatter plot demonstrates patterns or clusters and highlights high-risk customers based on satisfaction and churn scores.
visualization.requirement: Implement a scatter plot of `satisfaction_score` versus `churn_score`, colored by CLTV tiers (e.g., low, medium, high).

visualization.reason: A box plot effectively depicts differences in churn scores between churned and non-churned customers, showcasing outliers and variability.
visualization.requirement: Create a box plot to compare the distribution of churn scores for customers who have churned versus stayed.

visualization.reason: A pie chart provides a quick and readily understandable overview of the proportions of customer statuses.
visualization.requirement: Introduce a pie chart to display the overall distribution of `customer_status` (Stayed, Churned, Joined).

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW view_overview AS SELECT Customer_Info.customer_id, Customer_Info.age, Customer_Info.senior_citizen, Customer_Info.partner, Customer_Info.dependents, Status_Analysis.satisfaction_score, Status_Analysis.cltv, Status_Analysis.churn_score FROM Customer_Info JOIN Status_Analysis ON Customer_Info.customer_id = Status_Analysis.customer_id;
sqls.name: view_overview

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW view_exploration AS SELECT Status_Analysis.churn_score, Status_Analysis.churn_value, Status_Analysis.customer_status FROM Status_Analysis;
sqls.name: view_exploration

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW view_detail AS SELECT Status_Analysis.satisfaction_score, Status_Analysis.churn_score, Status_Analysis.cltv FROM Status_Analysis;
sqls.name: view_detail

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW view_insight AS SELECT Customer_Info.age, Status_Analysis.satisfaction_score, Status_Analysis.cltv, Status_Analysis.churn_score FROM Customer_Info JOIN Status_Analysis ON Customer_Info.customer_id = Status_Analysis.customer_id;
sqls.name: view_insight

tasks.method: Merge `Customer_Info` and `Status_Analysis` tables on `customer_id` and create a grid-based visualization such as a segmented bar chart or grouped bar chart to represent demographic group distributions alongside satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV bins.
tasks.objective: Combine demographic details with key metrics to observe patterns and assess potential predictors of churn.
tasks.type: retrieving values | combining | determining ranges | characterizing distributions

tasks.method: Compute macro-level churn rate metrics (e.g., percentage of churned customers, mean churn score) and visualize the results using a pie chart (distribution of customer statuses) and bar chart (churn score distribution).
tasks.objective: Provide an overview of churn behaviors and trends to highlight high-risk patterns for targeted actions.

tasks.method: Create a heat map to show correlations among numerical variables, segmented by demographic groups such as age categories and partner status.
tasks.objective: Explore the relationships between satisfaction, churn, and revenue metrics across various customer demographics for actionable insights.

tasks.method: Plot a scatter plot of `satisfaction_score` versus `churn_score`, with points colored by CLTV tiers (e.g., low, medium, high) and hover interactions to display individual details.
tasks.objective: Identify clusters of high-risk and high-value customers, and observe patterns or anomalies in the interaction between satisfaction and churn scores.

tasks.method: Generate a box plot comparing churn score distributions for customers who have churned versus stayed, incorporating drill-down capabilities for detailed explorations.
tasks.objective: Compare the variability and central tendency of churn scores for churned and non-churned customers, highlighting significant differences.

tasks.method: Group customers into bins based on age (e.g., under 30, 30-50, 50+), CLTV levels, and churn risk, and visualize the results through stacked bar charts. Enable dynamic age and CLTV binning.
tasks.objective: Cluster customers into actionable categories for predictive modeling and highlight high-risk segments for retention priorities.

tasks.method: Visualize churn reasons and categories using a horizontal bar chart, sorted by frequency and integrated with drill-down to explore specific customer data.
tasks.objective: Understand the most frequent reasons behind churn to refine and prioritize targeted intervention strategies.

tasks.method: Plot a multi-line time series or segmented bar chart showing trends of monthly charges and total revenue for customers grouped by churn score bins.
tasks.objective: Identify if customers at higher churn risk contribute significantly to revenues and monitor trends for early intervention.
tasks.type: retrieving values | computing derived values | correlating
